text:
Olympic athlete Jackson James Rice has tragically died aged 18 in a diving accident just weeks before he was set to make history at the Paris Games this summer.

It is understood that the teenager died in Faleloa, Tonga on Saturday from a "suspected shallow water blackout". Rice had been free diving from a boat when the tragic incident occurred.

His body was discovered beneath the boat at 12.15pm local time by other divers, with subsequent resuscitation attempts unsuccessful.

READ MORE:Rose of Tralee contestant reported missing by family to New Zealand police found dead

Rice’s father Darren Rice confirmed his son’s death to theMatangi Tonga newspaper.

Tongan athlete Pita Taufatofua, who served as Tonga’s flagbearer at the 2016 Rio Olympics, was among those to pay tribute to the teen.

“In between helping at the resort, he would do what he loved - kite surfing - out in the oceans of Ha’apai," Taufatofua wrote in a post shared on Facebook.

“Being self-taught as a kid, he reached a level where he would compete at the recent Olympic-qualifiers on his quest to one day represent Tonga at the Olympic Games.

“He had a great respect for the sea and for all the people in our little motu [island]. JJ left this world doing exactly what he loved, in a place he loved - the ocean.”

Rice's sister Lily also penned an emotional tribute to Rice on Facebook, writing: "I was blessed with the most amazing brother in the whole world and it pains me to say that he’s passed away.

"He was an amazing kitefoiler and he would have made it to the Olympics and come out with a big shiny medal … he made so many amazing friends all over the world."

Rice was due to become the first caucasian to represent Tonga at next month’s Games, where the sport he specialised in - kite foiling - is to make its debut.

Kite foil racing is similar to kitesurfing, but uses a foil board, so the rider appears to fly above the water and can perform acrobatic tricks and quick turns

Rice was born in the United States, but has lived his entire life in Tonga, where his parents, who are originally from the UK, own an island resort in Faleloa.

The teenager qualified for what had been due to be his first Olympic Games last December, courtesy of an eight-placed finish at a Sail Sydney event in Australia.

title: Teenage Olympics star dies weeks before he was set to make history at Paris 2024 games - Irish Mirror
author: Ruairi Scott Byrne
description: Jackson James Rice was set to represent Tonga at next month’s Olympic Games, where the sport he specialised in - kite foiling - is to make its debut
